channel.title: Drupal AI
channel.description: Stay informed about developments in Drupal AI.

item.title: #3583809: Add Text Classification support
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.0.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 3 files, +52/−5 · php +51/−4 · json +1/−1
  • Diff: 2b16e6b
  • Discussion: #3583809 · 6 contributors · 18 comments
  • Followers: 8

Summary

The ai_provider_huggingface module now supports the text_classification operation type introduced by the AI core module. Site builders can add a Hugging Face text-classification endpoint (such as distilbert/distilbert-base-uncased-finetuned-sst-2-english) on the provider settings page and then use the Text Classification Explorer at /admin/config/ai/explorers/text_classification_generator. The module's drupal/ai dependency has been raised to ^1.4.x-dev@dev, which is the version that ships the text-classification operation type classes.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Requirements: Minimum drupal/ai version raised from ^1.3.x-dev@dev to ^1.4.x-dev@dev.
  • New API: New text_classification operation type available in HuggingfaceProvider, implementing TextClassificationInterface from drupal/ai 1.4.x.

Technical details

The HuggingfaceProvider class now implements TextClassificationInterface and lists text_classification in both its $supportedTypes map (with Hugging Face pipeline filter text-classification) and its getSupportedOperationTypes() return value. The new textClassification() method accepts a string or TextClassificationInput, calls HuggingfaceApi::textClassification(), decodes the JSON response, handles both single-item and array responses from the Inference API, and wraps each result in a TextClassificationItem before returning a TextClassificationOutput.

A pre-existing bug in HuggingfaceApi::textClassification() was fixed at the same time: the method always included 'parameters' => [] in the request payload. PHP's json_encode serializes an empty array as [] (a JSON list), which the Hugging Face Inference API rejects with a 400 error and the message 'list' object has no attribute 'setdefault'. The fix omits the parameters key entirely when the array is empty; when parameters are present they are cast to (object) so they serialize as a JSON object {}.

The composer.json dependency on drupal/ai was bumped from ^1.3.x-dev@dev to ^1.4.x-dev@dev because TextClassificationInterface, TextClassificationInput, TextClassificationItem, and TextClassificationOutput were added to AI core in 1.4.x via #3456768.

Upgrade

If your project depends on ai_provider_huggingface, update your composer.json to require at least drupal/ai ^1.4.x-dev@dev:

"require": {
    "drupal/ai": "^1.4.x-dev@dev"
}

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 9, 2026
  • Committed: July 10, 2026 (3 months later)

Collaboration

During review, csakiistvan identified that the original MR had four failing CI jobs: the TextClassification* classes did not exist in the drupal/ai 1.3.x branch declared in composer.json, causing non-ignorable phpstan errors. csakiistvan filed a detailed testing report documenting the root cause and the two fixes needed (dependency bump and use statement reordering). After ahmad khader applied those fixes, jucs7 re-tested the full flow, confirmed CI was green and the empty-parameters 400 error was resolved, and moved the issue to RTBC.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3548526: Support File Handling
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 4 files, +572/−2 · php +571/−1 · json +1/−1
  • Diff: 623d27c
  • Discussion: #3548526 · 6 contributors · 26 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

The OpenAI provider module for Drupal now supports the OpenAI Files API through the normalized AI module file-handling interface. Developers can upload files to OpenAI, attach them by remote ID to chat messages, download file content, and delete remote files. This enables use cases like document Q&A and fine-tuning data management without leaving the Drupal AI abstraction layer. Requires drupal/ai 1.4.2 or higher.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Requirements: Minimum drupal/ai version raised from ^1.4.0 to ^1.4.2
  • New API: OpenAiProvider now implements AiFileProviderInterface, adding uploadFile(), deleteFile(), downloadFile(), and supportsMimeType() methods for interacting with the OpenAI Files API.

Technical details

OpenAiProvider now implements AiFileProviderInterface (from drupal/ai 1.4.2+) and uses the FileApiTrait (Drupal\ai\Traits\OpenAi\FileApiTrait) to fulfil that interface. The trait provides uploadFile(), deleteFile(), downloadFile(), and supportsMimeType() methods backed by the OpenAI PHP SDK. MIME type eligibility is gated by purpose: batch and fine_tune accept text and JSON only, vision accepts image types, and user_data is unrestricted.

On the chat side, OpenAiProvider::chat() was extended to detect getRemoteFiles() on each ChatMessage and append {'type': 'file', 'file': {'file_id': $id}} content blocks to the outgoing API payload. This allows previously uploaded files to be referenced in a conversation turn without re-sending file bytes.

Error handling in uploadFile() maps "Request too large" API errors to AiRateLimitException and quota errors to AiQuotaException. The minimum drupal/ai constraint in composer.json was tightened from ^1.4.0 to ^1.4.2, which is the release that introduced AiFileProviderInterface and FileApiTrait in the base module (see #3546954).

Upgrade

No changes required for existing code. To use file handling, obtain the provider as AiFileProviderInterface and call its methods:

use Drupal\ai\AiFileProviderInterface;

// Upload a file (returns the AiFile entity with remote ID set).
/** @var AiFileProviderInterface $provider */
$aiFile = $this->aiFileManager->upload('public://docs/report.pdf', 1, 'openai');

// Reference the uploaded file in a chat message.
$message = new ChatMessage('user', 'Summarize this document.');
$message->addRemoteFile($aiFile->getRemoteId());
$input = new ChatInput([$message]);
$response = $provider->chat($input, 'gpt-4o')->getNormalized();

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: September 24, 2025
  • Committed: July 7, 2026 (9 months later)

Collaboration

b_sharpe demonstrated the feature working end-to-end in a comment showing file upload followed by referencing the remote ID in a chat message. marcus_johansson steered the implementation toward using the AiModelCapability enum for capability checks and suggested moving the file trait into the base AI module rather than keeping it in the provider, which is what ultimately shipped. b_sharpe subsequently rebased the MR against the 1.3.x branch after drupal/ai 1.4.2 landed with the normalized file API.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3600886: Add an "AI audio" field validation constraint (speech-to-text)
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 10 files, +328/−8 · php +322/−7 · md +6/−1
  • Diff: 94e5c2d
  • Discussion: #3600886 · 2 contributors · 11 comments
  • Followers: 3

Summary

The ai_validations module gains a seventh field validation rule: AI audio constraint. Site builders can now attach this rule to a File field that accepts audio uploads. When an editor saves a node, the constraint first transcribes the uploaded audio through a configurable speech-to-text provider, then evaluates that transcript with a separate chat provider using the same XTRUE/XFALSE prompt protocol the existing text and image constraints already use. If the chat provider returns XFALSE, the node is rejected and the configured error message is shown.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New transcribeAudio() and buildProviderDefaultValue(string $configKey) helpers on AiConstraintValidatorBase and AiConstraintFieldValidationRuleBase; loadImageFile() renamed to loadFile() on AiConstraintValidatorBase.

Technical details

Three new files implement the feature: AiAudioConstraintFieldValidationRule (the @FieldValidationRule plugin with id ai_audio_constraint_rule), AiAudioConstraint (the Symfony @Constraint with id AiAudioPrompt), and AiAudioConstraintValidator (the validator). The validator resolves both provider options, calls transcribeAudio() to get a string, then passes it straight into runBooleanChat() to obtain a boolean verdict.

Two base-class changes support the new plugin and clean up the existing ones. In AiConstraintFieldValidationRuleBase, the private logic formerly in buildModelDefaultValue() was extracted into a new buildProviderDefaultValue(string $configKey) method. buildModelDefaultValue() now delegates to it, so subclasses that need a second provider selector (as AiAudioConstraintFieldValidationRule does for its chat_provider key) can call buildProviderDefaultValue() with any config key without duplicating the decode logic. In AiConstraintValidatorBase, loadImageFile() was renamed to loadFile() to reflect that it handles any file type, and a new transcribeAudio(mixed $data, string $option) helper was added alongside the existing loadFile() and runBooleanChat() helpers. All three existing image-constraint validators (AiImageConstraintValidator, AiImageClassificationConstraintValidator, AiObjectDetectionConstraintValidator) were updated to call loadFile() instead of loadImageFile().

Upgrade

Custom validators that extend AiConstraintValidatorBase and call loadImageFile() must rename the call to loadFile():

// Before
$file = $this->loadImageFile($data);

// After
$file = $this->loadFile($data);

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 15, 2026
  • Committed: July 1, 2026 (16 days later)

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)
  • abhisekmazumdar (Dropsolid)

Collaboration

abhisekmazumdar documented two design choices beyond the original plan in comment #16647960: exposing two independent ai_provider_configuration selectors (speech-to-text and chat) rather than a single one, because the two operation types are commonly served by different providers, and adding transcribeAudio() to the shared base class rather than scoping it to the new validator alone, so future constraints can reuse it without duplication. marcus_johansson reviewed the patch, suggested one minor refinement, and marked it ready to commit.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3595515: Add AI Text Classification field validation rule (mirror of AI Image Classification)
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 1 file, +1/−0 · php +1
  • Diff: c0270a8
  • Discussion: #3595515 · 2 contributors · 18 comments
  • Followers: 3

Summary

The ai_validations module now ships a fifth field validation rule: AI text classification constraint. Site owners can attach it to any text or string field and configure an AI provider model, a classification tag to block (e.g. "spam"), a matching strategy (exact, contains, or case-insensitive contains), a minimum confidence threshold (0–1), a "model not available" behavior (skip or fail), and a custom error message. When a content form is submitted, the validator sends the field value to the chosen AI provider's text-classification operation and adds a violation if the returned label matches the configured tag at or above the confidence threshold. This mirrors the behavior of the existing AI image classification rule.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New AiTextClassificationConstraint, AiTextClassificationConstraintValidator, and AiTextClassificationConstraintFieldValidationRule plugin classes; resolveProviderOption() in AiConstraintValidatorBase gains an optional $violateOnMissing bool parameter (default TRUE, fully backward-compatible).

Technical details

Three new plugin files implement the feature. AiTextClassificationConstraintFieldValidationRule (plugin id ai_text_classification_constraint_rule) extends AiConstraintFieldValidationRuleBase and exposes the config form; it returns 'AiTextClassification' from getConstraintName(). AiTextClassificationConstraint is the Symfony @Constraint class (id AiTextClassification) holding properties message, model, tag, finder, minimum, and na. AiTextClassificationConstraintValidator extends AiConstraintValidatorBase; its validate() method wraps the field value in TextClassificationInput, calls $provider->textClassification($input, $model), and iterates the normalized result through the shared labelMatchesFinder() / confidence comparison logic inherited from the base class.

During development a bug was found: when no text_classification provider was configured and the rule was set to na: skip, the validator was still raising a violation because resolveProviderOption() in AiConstraintValidatorBase unconditionally called addViolation() on a missing provider. The fix adds a $violateOnMissing boolean parameter (default TRUE) to resolveProviderOption(); the text classification validator passes $constraint->na !== 'skip' so the violation is suppressed when graceful degradation is requested.

Test coverage is provided by a new AiTextClassificationConstraintValidatorTest Kernel test. Because AiProviderPluginManager is declared final and cannot be mocked directly, a helper testing module (ai_validations_test) was added, containing ClassifierTestProvider (extending EchoProvider) whose textClassification() output is driven by Drupal state. This lets the Kernel test exercise every branch: match above threshold, match below threshold, label mismatch, contains-finder, empty value short-circuit, missing model with na: fail, provider exception with na: fail, and provider exception with na: skip. A FunctionalJavascript test case was also added to assert the selected model persists correctly through the config form save cycle.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 10, 2026
  • Committed: July 1, 2026 (20 days and 5 commits later)

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)
  • abhisekmazumdar (Dropsolid)

Collaboration

During MR review, abhisekmazumdar discovered a runtime bug: with na set to skip, the validator still raised a violation when no provider was found, because the shared resolveProviderOption() helper always called addViolation(). They described the root cause in a comment and fixed it by adding a $violateOnMissing parameter to resolveProviderOption() so callers can suppress the violation when graceful degradation is configured. They also documented why a Unit test was not viable (the final AiProviderPluginManager) and why they introduced the ai_validations_test helper module instead.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3600885: Add an "AI moderation" field validation constraint
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 3 files, +25/−2 · php +25/−2
  • Diff: 6abd71b
  • Discussion: #3600885 · 2 contributors · 20 comments
  • Followers: 3

Summary

The ai_validations module gains a fifth field validation rule: "AI moderation constraint." Site builders can attach it to any text field and it will call the AI module's native moderation() operation on form save, blocking submission when the provider flags the content as harmful. The rule's configuration lets you choose a provider and model, optionally restrict failures to specific moderation categories (e.g. hate,violence,sexual), and set a confidence threshold for those categories. If the moderation provider throws an exception, the error is logged and the field fails validation rather than crashing the site.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New ai_moderation_constraint_rule field validation rule plugin that screens text fields using the AI module's moderation() operation type.

Technical details

Three new classes implement the constraint following the existing plugin pattern. AiModerationConstraintFieldValidationRule (plugin id ai_moderation_constraint_rule) provides the admin config form with an ai_provider_configuration element for provider/model selection, a free-text categories field (comma-separated, blank means any flagged category triggers a violation), and a threshold float field (0–1, default 0.5) applied when specific categories are configured. AiModerationConstraint is the constraint data class holding $categories, $provider, and $threshold. AiModerationConstraintValidator resolves the provider, skips empty/null values using $data === NULL || (string) $data === '' (fixing a prior empty() false-negative on 0), then calls $provider->moderation(new ModerationInput($data), $model)->getNormalized(). If isFlagged() is true and no category filter is set, a violation is added immediately. With categories configured, the validator reads scores from getInformation() and adds a violation only when a watched category's score is numeric and exceeds the configured threshold. Provider exceptions are caught, logged via the ai_validations logger channel, and treated as a violation.

A separate fix in AiConstraintValidatorBase::runBooleanChat() wraps the $provider->chat() call in a try/catch that returns FALSE on any exception. Previously, providers that reject inputs via their own safety filter (before returning a response) would throw an uncaught exception and produce a 500 page; all three existing chat-based validators (text prompt, image prompt, audio) are affected by this fix.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 15, 2026
  • Committed: July 1, 2026 (16 days and 5 commits later)

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)
  • abhisekmazumdar (Dropsolid)

Collaboration

abhisekmazumdar contributed the implementation and noted a deliberate design choice in a comment: the original issue proposed a confidence threshold, but getInformation() returns provider-dependent structures (floats on OpenAI, different shapes elsewhere), so a cross-provider numeric threshold was initially skipped. After review rounds with marcus_johansson, the threshold was re-added with proper is_numeric() guarding so it only applies when the value is a comparable float. abhisekmazumdar also caught and fixed a separate fatal-error bug in runBooleanChat() while preparing a demo, which was rolled into the same branch.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3593019: Add ImageToImage possibilities
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +183/−1 · php +149/−1 · yml +34
  • Diff: e0a64ca
  • Discussion: #3593019 · 4 contributors · 18 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

The OpenAI AI provider module now supports the image_to_image operation type, enabling developers to use OpenAI's image editing endpoint (used by gpt-image-1 and dall-e-2) to modify existing images with a text prompt and an optional mask. Previously the module only supported generating images from text (text_to_image). Sites that want to offer image editing functionality through the AI module ecosystem can now wire up these models without building a custom provider.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New image_to_image operation type on OpenAiProvider, implementing ImageToImageInterface with support for prompt-driven image editing and optional mask via OpenAI's images-edit endpoint.

Technical details

OpenAiProvider now implements ImageToImageInterface and uses ImageToImageTrait. The new imageToImage() method only accepts an ImageToImageInput object (plain string or array inputs throw AiResponseErrorException), which carries the source image binary, an optional mask ImageFile, and the edit prompt. Because the OpenAI PHP SDK uploads files as multipart form data, the method writes the image (and mask, if supplied) to temporary:// paths via fileSystem->saveData(), opens them as PHP file resources, and passes them to $this->client->images()->edit(). A finally block guarantees the temporary files and open handles are cleaned up regardless of outcome. The response bodies are decoded from base64 JSON; output MIME type defaults to image/png but switches to image/jpeg or image/webp when output_format is set in the configuration payload. The provider declares requiresImageToImagePrompt() as TRUE (the edits endpoint always requires a prompt) and hasImageToImageMask() as TRUE. Model filtering in the provider's model-listing logic restricts image_to_image to models matching dall-e-2 or gpt-image-*, explicitly excluding dall-e-3 which cannot edit images. The default model recommendation for this operation type is gpt-image-1. api_defaults.yml gained an image_to_image section covering n (1–10) and size configuration keys.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 1, 2026
  • Committed: June 26, 2026 (24 days later)

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)
  • merilainen (Mearra (formerly known as Wunder))

Collaboration

During review, merilainen approved the change and made a small follow-up code adjustment, removing the response_format key from the image_to_image section of api_defaults.yml after marcus_johansson flagged it as incorrect. merilainen then marked the issue RTBC and marcus_johansson merged it.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3590873: Dall-E 3 has been deprecated and should be removed as default
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major bug report
  • Version: 1.2.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 3 files, +29/−4 · php +24/−1 · yml +5/−3
  • Discussion: #3590873 · 4 contributors · 13 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

OpenAI deprecated the Dall-E 3 image model on 12 May 2026. This change updates the AI Provider OpenAI module to replace dall-e-3 with gpt-image-1 as the default text_to_image model, and revises the available image sizes to match those supported by gpt-image-1. Existing sites that had dall-e-3 set automatically as their default will be migrated to gpt-image-1 when they run database updates. Sites where an administrator explicitly chose dall-e-3 are not affected by the update hook.

Impact

Affects site owners.

  • Database update: ai_provider_openai_update_10002 migrates any stored dall-e-3 default to gpt-image-1 in ai.settings.
  • Config change: Default text_to_image model in ai.settings changes from dall-e-3 to gpt-image-1; available size options updated to match gpt-image-1 (removes 256x256 and 512x512, adds portrait and landscape variants).

Technical details

In OpenAiProvider, the hardcoded default for the text_to_image operation changes from 'dall-e-3' to 'gpt-image-1'. The accompanying configuration schema removes the Dall-E 3 size options (256x256, 512x512) and replaces them with the sizes gpt-image-1 supports: 1024x1024, 1024x1536, 1536x1024, 1024x1792, and 1792x1024, with a new default of 1024x1024.

A new ai_provider_openai_update_10002() hook handles existing installations. It reads ai.settings and checks whether default_providers['text_to_image']['model_id'] is exactly dall-e-3. If so, it rewrites that key to gpt-image-1 and saves the config. Sites that manually selected dall-e-3 retain their stored value; the hook only replaces the old auto-assigned default.

Upgrade

Run database updates after installing the new module version:

drush updb

If your site had dall-e-3 set as the automatic default, it will be migrated to gpt-image-1. If you explicitly configured dall-e-3 and wish to continue using it while OpenAI still supports the model, no automatic change will be made, but you should plan to select a supported model before OpenAI fully removes dall-e-3.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 19, 2026

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)
  • abhisekmazumdar (Dropsolid)
  • danrod (OPTASY)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3604032: Add static_list completion providers to example prompt argument configs and cover them with tests
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3604032 · 1 contributor · 3 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

The mcp_server_examples module now ships example prompt argument configurations that expose static_list completion providers, enabling MCP clients to autocomplete valid values for arguments such as skill_level, court_surface, focus_area, and others. Previously these arguments described their valid values only in prose; they now carry machine-readable completion data that MCP clients can query. Functional test coverage was added at the same time to verify each configured argument through the completion/complete endpoint.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

Each affected prompt config YAML received one or more completion_providers blocks using plugin_id: static_list, listing the exact accepted values. The affected arguments span six prompt configs: comprehensive_lesson.lesson_focus, match_strategy.opponent_type and court_surface, multi_provider_prompt.opponent_type (which was also missing three of its four values), tennis_resources.topic, tennis_tip_basic.skill_level and focus_area, and test_with_args.skill_level and surface. No schema changes were needed because mcp_server.mcp_prompt_config.* already declared completion_providers as a supported key.

The new assertPromptArgumentCompletions() method in McpServerExamplesFunctionalTest iterates over nine argument cases, calls the completion/complete MCP endpoint with an empty argument value, and asserts that completion.values, completion.total, and completion.hasMore all match the configured static list. The method is called from the existing testMcpServerExamples() entry point, so it runs as part of the standard functional test suite.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 18, 2026

Key contributors

  • e0ipso (Acquia)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3586397: Switch FieldValidationRule plugins to the Provider Configuration Form Element
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 14 files, +856/−449 · php +851/−449 · neon +4 · txt +1
  • Diff: 66d9d6f
  • Discussion: #3586397 · 4 contributors · 23 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

The AI Validations module previously used a plain dropdown for selecting a specific AI provider and model in each validation rule, with no way to fall back to the site's configured default provider. This made it impossible to create portable recipes or shared configurations without hardcoding a provider. Each of the four AI FieldValidationRule plugins now uses the AI module's reusable ai_provider_configuration form element, which surfaces a "Default" option that automatically falls back to whichever provider the site administrator has configured as the default for the relevant operation type. Existing saved rule configurations using a hardcoded provider__model string continue to work without any data migration.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: Two new public abstract base classes: AiConstraintFieldValidationRuleBase for rule plugins and AiConstraintValidatorBase for constraint validators, intended for custom AI validation rule implementations.

Technical details

Two new abstract base classes consolidate the shared code that was previously duplicated across each plugin. AiConstraintFieldValidationRuleBase (in src/AiConstraintFieldValidationRuleBase.php) extends ConstraintFieldValidationRuleBase and centralizes ai.provider service injection, plus the helpers buildModelDefaultValue() (decodes a stored provider__model string into the structured array the ai_provider_configuration element expects as #default_value) and extractStoredModel() (normalizes the submitted element value back into a provider__model string or empty string). Subclasses implement the abstract getOperationType() method and may override getModelConfigKey() to preserve legacy config key names: AiTextConstraintFieldValidationRule and AiImageConstraintFieldValidationRule store under provider, while AiImageClassificationConstraintFieldValidationRule and AiObjectDetectionConstraintFieldValidationRule use model.

AiConstraintValidatorBase (in src/AiConstraintValidatorBase.php) extends ConstraintValidator and provides three shared helpers used by all four validators: resolveProviderOption() resolves the stored provider__model option or falls back to getDefaultProviderForOperationType() on the ai.provider plugin manager; loadImageFile() loads a file entity by ID with consistent violation handling; runBooleanChat() encapsulates the XTRUE/XFALSE prompt protocol; and labelMatchesFinder() applies the exact/contains/substring matching strategies. A previously missed gap was fixed during review: AiObjectDetectionConstraintValidator was still reading $constraint->model directly instead of calling resolveProviderOption(), meaning it could not fall back to the configured default. It now matches the other three validators.

A new hook_form_FORM_ID_alter() in ai_validations.module (with submit handler _ai_validations_sync_rule_to_collection()) works around a latent field_validation bug exposed when the AJAX-driven ai_provider_configuration element is present: serialization during the AJAX round-trip causes the form controller's plugin instance to diverge from the rule set's plugin collection, silently dropping edits on final save. The workaround uses ReflectionProperty to push the form's modified instance back into the collection before save(). A @todo links to the upstream issue #3587461 for removal once the bug is fixed there.

Upgrade

Developers who have subclassed any of the four rule plugins directly should update their extends clause to use the new base class (or ConstraintFieldValidationRuleBase if the AI-specific helpers are not needed). The stored configuration format (provider__model string, or empty string for "Default") is unchanged, so no data migration is required.

// Before (example for a custom plugin extending one of the AI rule plugins):
class MyCustomAiRule extends AiTextConstraintFieldValidationRule { ... }

// After (extend the new shared base directly if you need provider config support):
class MyCustomAiRule extends AiConstraintFieldValidationRuleBase {
  protected function getOperationType(): string {
    return 'chat'; // return the relevant operation type
  }
}

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 23, 2026
  • Committed: June 12, 2026 (1 month later)

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)
  • ahmad-khalil-imagex
  • a.dmitriiev (1xINTERNET)
  • abhisekmazumdar (Dropsolid)

Collaboration

During review, abhisekmazumdar noticed that AiObjectDetectionConstraintValidator had been left out of the resolveProviderOption() migration: it was still reading $constraint->model directly, meaning object detection rules could not fall back to the site's default provider even after the other three validators were updated. abhisekmazumdar added the fix alongside a clarifying doc comment explaining why $strictConfigSchema = FALSE was set in the test class. ahmad-khalil-imagex also added substantial functional JavaScript test coverage after a.dmitriiev requested it, including a detailed account in comment #12 of an AJAX ordering gotcha: the provider select fires a rebuild that wipes sibling field values, so the helper primeRuleFormFields() must populate required fields before the provider is selected.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3595519: Depend on core recipe for image media type
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.0.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +1/−2 · yml +1/−1 · json +0/−1
  • Diff: b9938de
  • Discussion: #3595519 · 2 contributors · 9 comments
  • Followers: 3

Summary

The ai_recipe_validations_image_safety recipe previously required the drupal_cms_media recipe as a dependency, which pulled in many unrelated packages and had a broken Composer version constraint (^1.0 while the package is now at 2.x). This made the recipe uninstallable via Composer. The dependency is now replaced with the leaner core/recipes/image_media_type recipe, which is what the recipe actually needs. The change also broadens compatibility so the recipe works on any Drupal site that uses the core image media type, not just Drupal CMS environments.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

In composer.json, the drupal/drupal_cms_media: ^1.0 require entry is removed entirely. In the recipe YAML, the recipes list entry drupal_cms_media is replaced with core/recipes/image_media_type. The drupal/core and other direct dependencies (drupal/ai, drupal/field_validation) are unchanged. The core/recipes/image_media_type recipe sets up the Media: Image bundle that this recipe's field validations target, so it satisfies the actual runtime requirement without the overhead of the full Drupal CMS media stack. A secondary effect noted in the thread is that the old ^1.0 constraint had no matching release (drupal_cms_media skipped 1.x entirely and went straight to 2.x), causing a hard Composer resolution failure before this fix.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 10, 2026
  • Committed: June 11, 2026 (1 day later)

Collaboration

a.dmitriiev noted in a follow-up comment that the change also resolved a concrete Composer installation failure: drupal/drupal_cms_media ^1.0 had no satisfying release because the package was only available at 2.x, making the recipe impossible to composer require at all on any current Drupal project.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3355087: Support for non-bundle entity types
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 2.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 27 files, +1120/−228 · php +1061/−223 · yml +51 · md +8/−5
  • Discussion: #3355087 · 9 contributors · 40 comments
  • Followers: 22

Summary

The Scheduler module now supports entity types that have no bundles, such as custom entities built without a bundle type or entities from contrib modules like ai_context. Previously, Scheduler silently dropped any plugin targeting a no-bundle entity type during discovery, making it impossible to schedule publishing on such entities regardless of the plugin definition. After this change, site owners can enable and configure scheduled publishing and unpublishing on no-bundle entity types via a new per-entity-type settings page at /admin/config/content/scheduler/{entity_type_id}. Scheduler 2.3.0 includes this feature.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Config change: New scheduler.no_bundle_entity_type_settings.* config objects store publish/unpublish settings for no-bundle entity types, reusing the scheduler_third_party_settings schema.
  • New API: New SchedulerManager methods: hasBundleType(), entityTypeNoBundleConfig(), getNoBundleEntityTypeSetting(), buildSchedulerFormElements(); new SchedulerNoBundleSettingsForm class and scheduler.no_bundle_settings_form route.

Technical details

The root blocker was in SchedulerPluginManager::findDefinitions(), which called $entityType->getBundleEntityType() and unset any plugin definition that returned falsy. Removing that guard opens discovery to no-bundle types.

With discovery unblocked, several other subsystems needed to branch on whether a bundle type exists. The new SchedulerManager::hasBundleType() method encapsulates that check. getThirdPartySetting() now delegates to getNoBundleEntityTypeSetting() for no-bundle entities, reading from scheduler.no_bundle_entity_type_settings.{entity_type_id} config objects rather than third-party settings on a bundle entity. getEnabledTypes() returns [$entityTypeId] when the no-bundle config enables scheduling, and the publish/unpublish entity queries skip the typeFieldName() bundle condition when typeFieldName() returns an empty string. Logging was refactored into the protected getEntityLogContext() helper to handle both paths without fatal bundle-entity loads.

The new SchedulerNoBundleSettingsForm provides a dedicated admin UI registered at the scheduler.no_bundle_settings_form route; settings are saved to scheduler.no_bundle_entity_type_settings.* config objects, which reuse the existing scheduler_third_party_settings config schema. Form-building logic was extracted from _scheduler_entity_type_form_alter() into the public SchedulerManager::buildSchedulerFormElements() helper to avoid duplication. A submit-time edge case is also handled: form modes registered only via hook_entity_form_mode_info_alter() with no backing entity_form_mode config entity ("phantom" modes) would cause a fatal in EntityDisplayBase::calculateDependencies(); the submit handler now skips such modes. SchedulerPluginBase::typeFieldName() returns an empty string for no-bundle types, and getTypes() and entityTypeFormIds() return early empty arrays when no bundle entity type exists.

A new scheduler_no_bundle_test module ships a scheduler_test_no_bundle entity type (EntityTestNoBundlePublishable) and its SchedulerTestNoBundleScheduler plugin. A SchedulerNoBundleSupportTest kernel test covers discovery, settings resolution, and the phantom form-mode edge case. The new SchedulerNoBundleEntitySetupTrait and dataNoBundleEntityTypes() data provider extend existing functional tests to exercise the no-bundle code path alongside bundled entity types.

Upgrade

To write a Scheduler plugin for a no-bundle entity type, declare a standard @SchedulerPlugin annotation omitting any bundle-specific fields. No typeFieldName() override is needed; the base class returns '' automatically.

/**
 * @SchedulerPlugin(
 *   id = "my_entity_scheduler",
 *   label = @Translation("My entity scheduler plugin"),
 *   description = @Translation("Provides scheduler support for my entity type"),
 *   entityType = "my_entity",
 *   dependency = "my_module"
 * )
 */
class MyEntityScheduler extends SchedulerPluginBase implements ContainerFactoryPluginInterface {}

Configure settings via the new config object rather than bundle third-party settings:

drush config-set scheduler.no_bundle_entity_type_settings.my_entity publish_enable 1
drush config-set scheduler.no_bundle_entity_type_settings.my_entity unpublish_enable 1

Or visit /admin/config/content/scheduler/my_entity in the admin UI. Existing code that reads settings through SchedulerManager::getThirdPartySetting() continues to work without changes; the method now routes no-bundle entities to getNoBundleEntityTypeSetting() automatically.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 19, 2023

Key contributors

  • nicodh
  • jonathan1055
  • mglaman (Acquia)
  • kristen pol (Itty Bitty Byte)
  • scott falconer (Acquia)
  • phenaproxima (Acquia)

Collaboration

mglaman opened the MR with a detailed audit of every bundle-assumption touch point in the 2.x codebase and a concrete five-step implementation plan. scott falconer identified a blocking crash: the SchedulerNoBundleSettingsForm submit handler looped over all form modes and attempted to save an EntityFormDisplay for modes registered only via hook_entity_form_mode_info_alter() without a backing config entity, triggering a fatal in EntityDisplayBase::calculateDependencies(). He documented the exact stack trace and noted that the config save happened before the loop, leaving sites partially configured on failure. mglaman addressed both problems in a follow-up commit. jonathan1055 reviewed the final MR, requested renaming the test class from EntityTestNoBundleScheduler to SchedulerTestNoBundleScheduler, explained why no-bundle entities cannot be wired into the existing JavaScript test suite (the JS tests configure scheduling via setThirdPartySetting() on a bundle object, which does not exist for no-bundle types), and merged and released Scheduler 2.3.0.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3588809: [Sprint 8] Add Phase 5.5 (asset library setup) using canvas:asset_library
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3588809 · 1 contributor · 5 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

The canvas_ai_migrations workflow gains a new Phase 5.5 that deploys global CSS and brand kit settings to Canvas config entities as a deterministic (non-LLM) step. Previously, global.css was bundled inside a specific component, meaning any style update required re-uploading the whole component. After this change, global styles live in the Canvas asset_library config entity and can be independently PATCH-updated post-migration without touching component bundles. A companion script also patches the global brand_kit entity with fonts discovered during migration. Both scripts run via Node.js and are orchestrated between Phase 5 (media) and Phase 6 (site-config) in SKILL.md.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

Two new scripts are added under scripts/:

setup-asset-library.mjs targets /canvas/api/v0/config/asset_library/global using the canvas:asset_library OAuth scope. It reads src/components/global.css, GETs the endpoint to check existence, then either POSTs (HTTP 201 expected) or PATCHes (HTTP 200 expected). The payload uses { id, label, css: { original, compiled } }. On completion it writes docs/migration/workflow/05.5-asset-library-checklist.json conforming to state-envelope.schema.json via an atomic .tmp-to-rename write.

setup-brand-kit.mjs targets /canvas/api/v0/config/brand_kit/global using the canvas:brand_kit scope. Live API probing during development confirmed that POST on the brand_kit endpoint returns 403 with "Brand kits cannot be created via the API" — only the pre-provisioned global instance exists per Acquia installation. The script therefore does GET-then-PATCH only. Fonts are read from docs/migration/discovery/design-tokens.json; if no fonts key is present, the script exits cleanly with a skip note. PATCH requires the Drupal permission administer brand kit; the script detects the 403 message and throws a permission-specific error. The canvas:brand_kit OAuth scope is only available on Acquia release 173672 and later; older instances will record invalid_scope in the scope-availability manifest and Phase 5.5 brand_kit step is skipped automatically.

Both scripts export a pure function with a fetchImpl parameter for fetch-mocked unit tests, and use a CLI guard (import.meta.url === file://${process.argv[1]}) so importing the module in tests does not trigger side effects. SKILL.md documents the two-script sequence, OAuth scope requirements, and the POST-disabled brand_kit constraint. component-builder.md adds a ## Global CSS handling note that components ship JS and component-scoped styles only. Two new entries in references/gotchas.md cover the brand_kit POST restriction and the administer brand kit permission requirement.

Upgrade

Operators running the canvas_ai_migrations workflow should add Phase 5.5 between media upload and site-config phases:

# Phase 5.5 — Asset Library + Brand Kit Setup
node scripts/setup-asset-library.mjs
node scripts/setup-brand-kit.mjs

Ensure the canvas:asset_library OAuth scope is granted (it is universally available on Canvas/Acquia). For brand kit setup, ensure the canvas:brand_kit scope is available (Acquia release 173672+) and that the migrating user role has the administer brand kit Drupal permission. If the permission is absent, setup-brand-kit.mjs exits 1 with a descriptive error; coordinate with the Acquia operator before running Phase 5.5.

Global CSS is now read from src/components/global.css and should no longer be embedded in any component bundle. The CANVAS_GLOBAL_CSS_PATH environment variable can override the default path.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 6, 2026

Collaboration

The closing comment from ajv009 notes that the brand_kit endpoint was not part of the original ticket scope. It was discovered through inspection of openapi.yml and confirmed via live API probing on 2026-05-17, which revealed the POST-disabled constraint, the PATCH-only contract, and the administer brand kit permission requirement. This empirical finding directly shaped the implementation contract and is why setup-brand-kit.mjs is PATCH-only with explicit permission-error detection.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3588808: [Sprint 8] Add Phase 1.5 (content model setup) using content_type and taxonomy scopes
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3588808 · 1 contributor · 5 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

Sites migrated with the canvas_ai_migrations migrate-site skill no longer require manually creating Drupal content types and taxonomy vocabularies before running Phase 7 (content composition). Previously, if a source site had dynamic content types (events, blog posts, news items), implementers had to create the corresponding Drupal bundles via the admin UI themselves before Phase 7 would work. The new Phase 1.5 handles this automatically using browser automation against the Drupal admin UI, since the JSON:API endpoints needed for a fully programmatic path are not yet available on Acquia Source instances.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New content_model_setup_completed state event documented in state-events.md for Phase 1.5 completion tracking.

Technical details

The change adds a ### Phase 1.5 — Content Model Setup (conditional) block to SKILL.md, inserted between Phase 1 (site-analyzer) and Phase 2 (component-builder). Phase 1.5 is skipped when plan.md contains no content_types: entries; it executes when Phase 1 detected dynamic content types.

The implementation uses agent-browser authenticated via the .canvas-browser/ session profile to navigate /admin/structure/types and /admin/structure/taxonomy in the Drupal admin HTML UI. For each content type declared in plan.md, the phase checks whether the bundle exists, creates it via the /admin/structure/types/add form if missing, and then provisions each declared field. Taxonomy vocabularies are handled similarly via /admin/structure/taxonomy/add. The JSON:API routes /jsonapi/node_type/* and /jsonapi/taxonomy_vocabulary/* remain 404 on all probed Acquia Source releases; the design explicitly documents them as the future API path, gated by scope-availability.json, for when Acquia ships those endpoints.

Phase 1.5 writes docs/migration/workflow/01.5-content-model-checklist.json per the Phase Gate Convention and appends a content_model_setup_completed event to state.jsonl. A new precondition in the Phase 7 block requires this checklist to exist with payload.overall: pass when any content_types: were declared; otherwise Phase 7 halts with a blocked.md entry. Five new scope probe entries (canvas:brand_kit, content_type:administer, taxonomy:administer, page_template:administer, site_settings:administer) are added additively to scripts/preflight/lib/scope-manifest.json, bringing the total to 14 scopes. The five historical acquia_source:* entries are preserved unchanged. Nine tests in tests/skills/sprint8-3588808-content-model-phase.test.mjs cover the new section, scope-manifest contents, state-events documentation, and the cross-file checklist filename consistency.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 6, 2026

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3588799: [Sprint 8] Canvas content templates as a phase for content-type migrations
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3588799 · 1 contributor · 5 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

The canvas_ai_migrations migrate-site skill now has a fully working Phase 4.5 for Canvas content template creation. The Sprint 6 stub agent referenced an admin UI path (/admin/structure/canvas-templates) that returns 404 on every probed Acquia Source instance. This refactor replaces it with the real Canvas REST API at /canvas/api/v0/config/content_template, using Drupal session cookies rather than OAuth (no OAuth scope exists for this endpoint). One template per content type now serves all current and future nodes of that type automatically, so content editors can publish new events, blog posts, or other dynamic items without any developer intervention or script run per node.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The canvas-content-template-agent.md was fully rewritten around a two-step API pattern. POST /canvas/api/v0/config/content_template with a minimum body of {entityType, bundle, viewMode} creates an empty draft entity with status: false; a subsequent PATCH /<id> with {elements, status: true} populates and activates it. On a 409 conflict (template already exists), the agent skips the POST and patches directly. All requests use Drupal session cookies from agent-browser --profile .canvas-browser/ combined with a CSRF token fetched as plain text from GET /session/token. The OAuth client-credentials path cannot be used for this endpoint.

Two Nebula discovery endpoints are now wired in before template construction: GET /canvas/api/v0/ui/content_template/view_modes/node confirms valid bundle/view-mode combinations, and GET /canvas/api/v0/ui/content_template/suggestions/prop-sources/node/<bundle>/<configEntityId> returns ready-to-paste prop source objects so that the agent does not need to manually construct the Unicode-separated entity-field expression syntax.

Phase 4.5 in SKILL.md now has two hard preconditions: the .canvas-browser/ profile must be authenticated (verified at Phase 0.5, with phase_4_5_blocked written to state.jsonl on failure), and the migrating Drupal user must have the administer content templates permission. On a 403 from the API, the agent writes a structured entry to journals/blocked.md and halts. The orchestrator gates progression on payload.overall: pass in the new JSON checklist at docs/migration/workflow/04.5-content-template-checklist.json. A new content_template_created state event is defined in state-events.md. A new reference file canvas-api-contracts.md documents all four Canvas API groups (js_component, asset_library, brand_kit, content_template) with their auth requirements, CRUD shapes, and common error codes, sourced from canvas openapi.yml and live empirical probes.

Upgrade

Operators running Phase 4.5 must take two actions before the migration session:

  1. Grant the administer content templates Drupal permission to the migrating user role. This permission is not assigned to default admin users and may not appear on older Acquia releases' permissions page. Confirm with the Acquia operator.

  2. Ensure the .canvas-browser/ agent-browser profile is authenticated before the migration session starts (via npm run verify-browser-session). Phase 4.5 will halt immediately if the session is stale.

There is no admin UI alternative. /admin/structure/canvas-templates and /admin/canvas/templates both return 404 on Acquia Source; the API at /canvas/api/v0/config/content_template is the only path.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 6, 2026

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3588798: [Sprint 8] Promote PASS/FAIL table format across all sub-agents
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3588798 · 1 contributor · 5 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

The canvas_ai_migrations module's AI-assisted migration workflow has standardized how sub-agents report their results. Five agents (storybook-qa, phase-verifier, visual-verifier, upload-verifier, component-builder) now produce a structured PASS/FAIL table in their chat reply AND write a JSON checklist file to docs/migration/workflow/ before returning. The orchestrator reads those JSON files to decide whether a phase passed, instead of parsing the agent's prose output. This makes phase gating deterministic across long sessions that trigger context compaction, where prose summaries were previously paraphrased and lost specific evidence values.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The motivation was a concrete compaction-survival problem: the LLM compaction algorithm's "Files and Artifacts" section quotes table rows verbatim but paraphrases prose paragraphs, so agents that narrated results like "fixed styling differences" left the orchestrator unable to confirm specific values in later iterations. Agents that used tables (the existing artifact-checker) preserved exact values across compaction boundaries.

Each of the five updated agent definition files (.claude/agents/*.md) gained a ## Output format section specifying: a | Check | Status | Details | return table capped at 20 lines, and a mandatory side-effect of writing a JSON checklist to docs/migration/workflow/<zero-padded-phase>-<agent>-checklist.json. The checklist schema nests results under a payload key (with overall, items[], and gaps[]) so the pre-existing checklist-gate.sh Stop hook, which reads .payload.overall via jq, handles the new checklists without modification.

SKILL.md gained a ## Phase Gate Convention section formalizing three steps: (1) verify the checklist file exists at the predicted path, (2) read payload.overall and proceed or re-dispatch the agent listed in payload.gaps[].rerun, (3) if the file is missing, dispatch artifact-checker in phase-N mode as a fallback synthesizer. The state-events.md reference file gained an agent_checklist_written event definition so the orchestrator can short-circuit existence checks when the event is present in state.jsonl. Fourteen Vitest tests in tests/skills/sprint8-3588798-checklist-convention.test.mjs verify the presence of the ## Output format section, the correct payload-nested schema, and the zero-padded filename convention in every affected agent file.

Upgrade

No external code changes are required. If you have custom orchestration scripts that parse sub-agent chat replies to determine phase success, update them to read payload.overall from the corresponding docs/migration/workflow/<phase>-<agent>-checklist.json file instead. Example for upload-verifier after Phase 4:

overall=$(jq -r '.payload.overall' docs/migration/workflow/04-upload-verifier-checklist.json)
if [ "$overall" = "pass" ]; then
  # proceed
fi

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 6, 2026

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3588793: [Sprint 6] Canvas content templates not used for content-type migrations
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3588793 · 1 contributor · 5 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

Sites migrated with the canvas_ai_migrations migrate-site skill can now support self-service content creation for dynamic content types such as events, blog posts, and news items. Previously, the skill treated all content as one-off Canvas page entities, meaning editors could not create a new event from the Drupal admin UI and have it render with Canvas styles without re-running a migration script. This feature adds a Phase 4.5 to the skill that detects dynamic content types during Phase 1 analysis, then creates Canvas content templates bound to Drupal node bundle display modes (node.<type>.full), so any newly created node of a flagged type renders correctly by default.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New .claude/agents/canvas-content-template-agent.md adapter agent and Phase 4.5 conditional phase added to the migrate-site skill's SKILL.md.

Technical details

The skill previously used only Canvas page entities (explicit per-item pages created by the migration), ignoring Canvas content templates, which are display-mode-level registrations (node.<type>.full) that automatically apply a Canvas component layout to any node of a given bundle. The fix has four parts.

Phase 1 detection was added to site-analyzer.md: it now flags dynamic content URL patterns (/blog/*, /events/*, /news/*, /people/*, paginated date-based paths) and writes template_needed: true entries into plan.md.

Phase 4.5 (conditional) was inserted into .claude/skills/migrate-site/SKILL.md between Phase 4 (upload) and Phase 5 (media). It skips entirely when plan.md has no template_needed: true entries. When it runs, it dispatches the new canvas-content-template-agent for each flagged type, verifies rendering by creating a test node via the admin UI, and deletes any legacy per-item Canvas pages created as temporary workarounds (archiving via path-alias change is explicitly avoided because it creates stale path_alias entries and permanent 301 redirect traps).

canvas-content-template-agent.md is a thin adapter agent (Sonnet model, low-effort setting) that reads Acquia Nebula's canvas-content-templates SKILL.md for format conventions and field-expression syntax, accepts a content type name and component-to-field mapping from the orchestrator, produces a template JSON file at content-templates/{entityType}.{bundle}.{viewMode}.json, validates it with npx canvas validate, uploads via browser automation (admin-cookie session, because the page_template:administer OAuth endpoints returned 404 during an empirical scope probe on 2026-05-01), and verifies by creating a test node. The agent switches to OAuth once scripts/verify-scopes.mjs reports those endpoints are live.

Nebula as a devDependency is the structural mechanism: package.json gains "acquia-nebula": "github:acquia/nebula" and a postinstall symlink (ln -sfn ../../node_modules/acquia-nebula/.agents/skills .claude/reference/nebula), making all 24 Nebula skills available at .claude/reference/nebula/ and refreshable via npm update.

A docs/migration/gotchas.md entry was added noting that dynamic content types require Canvas content templates and that new content requires Phase 4.5, not a manual script. Four tests in tests/skills/sprint6-skill-architecture.test.mjs cover the Nebula symlink, agent file, Phase 4.5 wiring, and site-analyzer detection. Commits: 8ef9c5c, 54718ae, 1a9b582.

Upgrade

No API-breaking changes. Existing migrations that processed only static pages are unaffected (Phase 4.5 skips when plan.md has no template_needed: true entries).

For projects that have already migrated a site with dynamic content types:

  1. Run npm install to pull Acquia Nebula as a devDependency and establish the .claude/reference/nebula/ symlink.
  2. Re-run Phase 1 analysis against the source site to populate template_needed: true entries in plan.md.
  3. Run Phase 4.5 manually or re-run the full skill; it will dispatch canvas-content-template-agent for each flagged type.
  4. Delete any per-item Canvas page entities that were created as workarounds (do not archive via path-alias change — see docs/migration/gotchas.md).

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 6, 2026

Collaboration

The fix was driven entirely by ajv009, who documented both the architecture gap and its resolution in detail. The problem itself was discovered not through the agent's own documentation research but when a user consulted an external contact with Acquia platform knowledge during a recorded session. That external discovery, flagged explicitly in the issue body as a signal of incomplete platform knowledge in the skill, shaped the decision to adopt Acquia's Nebula skill as an updatable upstream rather than building a custom implementation from scratch.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3588588: Add Gemini CLI backend for harness-native plugin install
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 2.2.1
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3588588 · 1 contributor · 5 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

The drupal_devkit tool now includes a Gemini CLI backend for harness-native plugin installation. Previously, Gemini users could only install skills through the marketplace path; there was no way to install harness-native plugins (the equivalent of what Claude Code users get via ClaudeCodePluginBackend). The new backend brings install, uninstall, and list operations to Gemini CLI, closing that gap. Note that only repositories shipping a gemini-extension.json manifest are supported, which is a Gemini CLI requirement rather than a devkit limitation.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New GeminiPluginBackend class in harness_plugins/gemini.py exposes install, uninstall, and list_installed operations for Gemini CLI extensions.

Technical details

GeminiPluginBackend is implemented in src/drupal_devkit/harness_plugins/gemini.py, following the same shell-out pattern as ClaudeCodePluginBackend. It delegates to the gemini extensions install, gemini extensions uninstall, and gemini extensions list CLI subcommands, parses JSON output, performs idempotent installs, and rolls back on failure. Repos without a gemini-extension.json manifest fail at install time with "Configuration file not found" from the Gemini CLI itself. Eleven unit tests in tests/harness_plugins/test_gemini_backend.py cover is_available, install happy path, already-installed detection, install failure, uninstall, list_installed (empty, malformed, and valid JSON with stderr-prefix handling), and info.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 6, 2026

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3588587: Add Codex backend for harness-native plugin install
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Version: 2.2.1
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 6 files, +810/−0 · py +794 · json +16
  • Diff: 8529e83
  • Discussion: #3588587 · 1 contributor · 5 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

The drupal-devkit tool now supports harness-native plugin installation for Codex CLI and Gemini CLI users, not just Claude Code. Before this change, the drupal-devkit harness-plugin install command had no available backend for Codex or Gemini users even though both harnesses were listed in the README as supported. The drupal-workflow marketplace entry is now available under all three harnesses.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: Two new plugin backends registered under type keys "codex-marketplace" and "gemini-extension" in the harness plugin registry

Technical details

The HarnessPluginBackend protocol in harness_plugins/protocol.py already defined the interface (is_available, install, uninstall, list_installed, info). This commit adds two concrete implementations alongside the existing ClaudeCodePluginBackend.

CodexPluginBackend (type "codex-marketplace") manages plugins by combining a CLI call to codex plugin marketplace add to register the upstream marketplace source with direct manipulation of ~/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json, the file Codex reads on startup. Because individual plugin install/list in Codex is TUI-only as of v0.130+, the backend writes plugin entries directly into that JSON file. If _register_plugin raises after a marketplace was added during the same run, the backend attempts a rollback via codex plugin marketplace remove; it correctly skips rollback when the marketplace was already present before the run.

GeminiPluginBackend (type "gemini-extension") delegates entirely to the gemini extensions install/uninstall/list subcommands available in Gemini CLI v0.38+. A notable quirk is that gemini extensions list -o json writes its JSON array to stderr rather than stdout, and may prefix it with non-JSON lines such as theme registration notices. The backend handles this with _parse_json_array, which strips a leading non-JSON prefix before attempting to decode.

Both backends are registered in harness_plugins/__init__.py under their respective type keys in the BACKENDS dict, and corresponding marketplace entries ("drupal-workflow-codex" and "drupal-workflow-gemini") are added to default_marketplaces.json so the registry can resolve them by harness + type.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 6, 2026
  • Committed: May 14, 2026 (8 days later)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3588780: [Sprint 3] Component revision tracking — before/current/after comparison
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +57/−1 · md +57/−1
  • Diff: 2aca47e
  • Discussion: #3588780 · 1 contributor · 8 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

The canvas_ai_migrations project's component-fixer had no way to verify that a fix actually improved a component's visual fidelity versus introducing a regression. This sprint adds a lightweight per-component revision tracking system: before and after each fix attempt, screenshots and computed styles are captured and compared to the original source baseline. The agent can now determine whether a change was converging (post-fix is more similar to source), diverging (regression), or unchanged (fix missed the right element), and it halts with a revert instruction on diverging results.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New capture-component.sh script and compare-three-way.mjs aggregator; component-fixer and storybook-qa agent workflows gain mandatory pre/post capture steps and a revisions/revisions.jsonl log per component.

Technical details

Three new artifacts implement the system. scripts/visual/capture-component.sh is a bash script that issues a single agent-browser batch call per phase (source, pre, or post). For source phase it writes docs/migration/components/<Name>/source.png and source-styles.json. For pre/post phases it writes revisions/<N>-{phase}.png, <N>-{phase}-styles.json, and <N>-{phase}-diff.json (a pixel-diff against source.png). Batching all four browser actions into one call avoids repeated browser startup overhead.

scripts/visual/compare-three-way.mjs is the aggregator. It reads the two diff JSONs and three styles JSONs, converts diffRatio (0 = identical) to pixel_similarity (1 = identical), classifies direction as converging/diverging/unchanged using a 0.02-point band, and emits a JSON object with revision, pixel_similarity_pre, pixel_similarity_post, direction, and css_deltas. Each css_deltas entry carries a regressed flag that is true only when the pre-fix state matched source and the post-fix state does not, distinguishing regressions from properties that were already wrong.

The agent workflow in .claude/agents/component-fixer.md gains Step 3a (call capture-component.sh --phase pre before any code change) and Step 5a (call capture-component.sh --phase post after upload, then run compare-three-way.mjs and append a revisions.jsonl line). On direction: "diverging" the agent reverts with git restore and emits BLOCKED: regression detected. .claude/agents/storybook-qa.md is updated to call capture-component.sh --phase source once when the first issue for a component is filed, seeding the stable source.png baseline. The visual-comparison skill docs gain a dedicated Three-way comparison section documenting the full capture-aggregate-interpret flow. Vitest tests for compare-three-way.mjs and bash smoke tests for capture-component.sh are included; a new visual test suite is registered in vitest.config.js.

Upgrade

No migration of existing components is required. To start tracking revisions for a component that already has issues, run the source-baseline capture once:

scripts/visual/capture-component.sh --component <Name> --revision 0 --phase source \
  --url <SOURCE_URL> --selector "<SECTION_SELECTOR>"

Subsequent component-fixer runs will then use --revision 1, --revision 2, etc. automatically (the agent derives N from the line count of revisions/revisions.jsonl). Components that have never had a storybook-qa issue filed since this change will need the source baseline captured manually before the fixer can produce a direction result.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 6, 2026
  • Committed: May 13, 2026 (6 days and 4 commits later)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3588776: [Sprint 3] Issue files missing before-fix screenshots — fixer working blind
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 1 file, +8/−0 · md +8
  • Diff: 112f31e
  • Discussion: #3588776 · 1 contributor · 7 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

In the canvas_ai_migrations AI-agent workflow, the storybook-qa agent was filing visual-mismatch issues without always capturing a built screenshot at filing time. This left the component-fixer agent working blind: it had no before-fix baseline to compare against and could report a fix as successful without any visual evidence. Three Sprint 3 commits close this gap by making screenshot evidence mandatory at every stage of the Phase 3.5 QA loop. storybook-qa now refuses to write an issue.md file unless the required source/built PNG pairs already exist on disk. component-fixer now verifies those PNGs are present and non-zero before starting any fix, and aborts with a BLOCKED status when they are missing. A new Phase 3.5 gate runs validate-screenshots.mjs across all open issue directories and fails the gate if any required PNG is absent or zero-byte.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The changes touch three agent instruction files and add a new validation script plus tests.

In .claude/agents/storybook-qa.md, a new "Issue Filing Protocol (mandatory)" block (lines 98–130) describes a five-step pre-filing sequence: determine the Viewport (desktop, mobile, or both), capture source and built PNGs at the matching element selector via agent-browser screenshot, capture computed styles as JSON, then verify every PNG is non-zero before writing issue.md. Failure at any capture step causes the agent to emit a issue_filing_skipped event to state.jsonl and abort filing that issue rather than writing an incomplete record. The required filenames are viewport-driven: source-desktop.png / built-desktop.png for desktop, source-mobile.png / built-mobile.png for mobile, both pairs for both.

In .claude/agents/component-fixer.md, a "Pre-fix screenshot guard (P1-6)" block (lines 69–75) requires the fixer to check the PNG siblings for every path in issue_paths before any fix attempt. If any required PNG is missing or zero-byte, the agent emits an action: fix_blocked state event to state.jsonl and aborts with BLOCKED: issue <NN-desc> missing screenshot evidence — re-dispatch storybook-qa to re-capture. The implementation deliberately chose a hard abort rather than the ticket's proposed silent re-dispatch, because a missing baseline indicates Phase 3.5 did not complete properly; silent recovery would mask the upstream failure.

In .claude/agents/artifact-checker.md, the Phase 3.5 gate now includes an issue-dir screenshot check. It runs node scripts/issue-files/validate-screenshots.mjs docs/migration/issues/ against every issue directory whose issue.md has Status: open or Status: fixing. The script (scripts/issue-files/validate-screenshots.mjs) reads the Viewport field from each issue.md, derives the required PNG filenames, and checks existence and non-zero size for each. A non-zero exit fails the gate and pastes the failure list into the artifact summary. Vitest tests for the script live in tests/issue-files/required-screenshots.test.mjs and cover pass, missing file, zero-byte, mobile-only viewport, and resolved-issue-skip cases.

Upgrade

If you are running the canvas_ai_migrations Phase 3.5 QA loop, expect the following behavior changes:

  1. storybook-qa will refuse to write issue.md when any required PNG is missing. Ensure the agent has access to a running Storybook server and the production site so it can complete captures during filing.

  2. component-fixer will abort with BLOCKED (rather than proceeding) when an issue directory is missing its screenshot evidence. Re-dispatch storybook-qa to re-capture, then re-invoke component-fixer.

  3. The Phase 3.5 gate now runs validate-screenshots.mjs automatically. To run it manually against an existing issues directory:

node scripts/issue-files/validate-screenshots.mjs docs/migration/issues/

A non-zero exit prints the failure list. Zero exit prints PASS: all open issues have required screenshots.

  1. Issue file screenshot fields use viewport-driven filename pairs (source-desktop.png / built-desktop.png, etc.) rather than the source_screenshot: / built_screenshot: YAML keys described in the ticket's spec. The gate logic keys off the **Viewport:** field in issue.md, not YAML keys.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 6, 2026
  • Committed: May 13, 2026 (6 days and 3 commits later)

Collaboration

In the closing comment, ajv009 documented two deliberate deviations from the ticket's literal spec: viewport-driven filenames instead of YAML source_screenshot/built_screenshot fields, and a hard BLOCKED abort in component-fixer instead of the spec's suggested "log warning and re-run storybook-qa." The rationale given for the abort behavior was that silent recovery would mask upstream Phase 3.5 failures, which is the underlying problem the issue was filed to solve.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.
